restore-without-rewriting-history.label: Recover from Everyday Mistakes
restore-without-rewriting-history.summary: Create supplied temporary mistakes and recover them without rewriting history.

sketch2.title: A Staged Mistake May Need Two Actions

try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.title: Create and Restore an Unstaged README Mistake
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.prompt: Open `community-site/README.md`. Find the exact line `Local events and resources.` and replace it with `Accidental rewrite.` Save the file. Type `git diff -- README.md` and press Enter. Then type `git restore README.md` and press Enter, followed by `git status --short`.
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.hint: Make only the supplied temporary edit, inspect it, and restore that path.

help.concept: git restore PATH discards an unstaged working-tree change to that path.
help.hint_1: Replace only the supplied README sentence.
help.hint_2: The existing commits must remain unchanged.

try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.solutionCode:
git diff -- README.md
git restore README.md
git status --short
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.instructions: Open `community-site/README.md`. Find the exact line `Local events and resources.` and replace it with `Accidental rewrite.` Save the file. Type `git diff -- README.md` and press Enter. Then type `git restore README.md` and press Enter, followed by `git status --short`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html rm -f -- community-site/notes/old-events.txt rmdir -- community-site/notes 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Organize community files' rm -f -- community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js rmdir -- community-site/dist 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'dist/ *.log ' > community-site/.gitignore git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Ignore generated output' mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s '' > community-site/dist/preview.js mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '' > community-site/debug.log

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html rm -f -- community-site/notes/old-events.txt rmdir -- community-site/notes 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Organize community files' rm -f -- community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js rmdir -- community-site/dist 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'dist/ *.log ' > community-site/.gitignore git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Ignore generated output' mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s '' > community-site/dist/preview.js mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '' > community-site/debug.log

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.title: Create, Stage, and Unstage a Style Mistake
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.prompt: Open `community-site/styles.css`. Replace all existing content with exactly `body { color: purple; }` on line 1, then save the file. Type `git add styles.css` and press Enter. Then type `git restore --staged styles.css` and press Enter. Do not restore the working copy yet.
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.hint: Create the supplied temporary edit, stage it, then remove only that path from the proposal.

help.concept: git restore --staged removes a path from the proposed snapshot but preserves its working-tree content.
help.hint_1: Replace the complete file with the one supplied line.
help.hint_2: styles.css should remain modified after unstaging.

try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.solutionCode:
git add styles.css
git restore --staged styles.css
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.instructions: Open `community-site/styles.css`. Replace all existing content with exactly `body { color: purple; }` on line 1, then save the file. Type `git add styles.css` and press Enter. Then type `git restore --staged styles.css` and press Enter. Do not restore the working copy yet.

try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.title: Restore the Remaining Working-Tree Mistake
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.prompt: The previous step left the exact `body { color: purple; }` edit unstaged in `community-site/styles.css`. Type `git restore styles.css` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.
try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.hint: Now restore the working-tree copy from the committed snapshot.

help.concept: After unstaging preserves the edited file, a separate working-tree restore removes the unwanted content.
help.hint_2: The final short status should be empty.

try_restore_without_rewriting_history_sk.instructions: The previous step left the exact `body { color: purple; }` edit unstaged in `community-site/styles.css`. Type `git restore styles.css` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

single-choice-1.title: Purpose of git restore
single-choice-1.prompt: What is the primary purpose of `git restore path` in this course?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Correct working-tree and staging mistakes at the right layer while leaving existing commits untouched.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To discard an unwanted working-tree edit

single-choice-2.title: Effect of git restore --staged
single-choice-2.prompt: What happens when you use `git restore --staged` on a file?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Correct working-tree and staging mistakes at the right layer while leaving existing commits untouched.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is removed from the staging area but changes are kept
options.b: The file is deleted from the repository
options.c: The file is committed to the repository
options.d: The file is reverted to the last committed state

multi-choice-1.title: Choosing the Correct Restore Command
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which commands can discard an unwanted working-tree edit and unstage a file?
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Correct working-tree and staging mistakes at the right layer while leaving existing commits untouched.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

drag-reorder-1.title: Discard a Change That Is Already Staged
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the safe correction steps for a file whose accidental rewrite is both staged and present in the working tree.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Correct working-tree and staging mistakes at the right layer while leaving existing commits untouched.
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t1: Remove the accidental version from the staging area
tokens.t2: Restore the working file from the recorded version

restore-sketch-0.title: Restore the Layer You Intend
restore-sketch-0.bodyMarkdown: `git restore path` replaces an unstaged working-tree edit with the committed version. It does not rewrite earlier commits.

restore-sketch-1.title: Unstage Without Losing the File
restore-sketch-1.bodyMarkdown: `git restore --staged path` removes a path from the proposed snapshot while preserving its current working-tree content.

restore-sketch-2.title: A Staged Mistake May Need Two Actions
restore-sketch-2.bodyMarkdown: When the same unwanted edit is staged and still present in the file, unstage it first, then restore the working copy. This keeps the recovery explicit and safe.
